target.rawSql: SELECT toUnixTimestamp64Milli(ts) AS time, concat('L', toString(from_level), ' → L', toString(to_level), ': ', arrayStringConcat(reasons, ', ')) AS text FROM analytics.degradation_transitions WHERE $__timeFilter(ts) ORDER BY ts

panels.title: Level (governor)
panels.description: AUTHORITATIVE published degradation level (ae_degradation_level): the governor's hysteresis-smoothed decision (enter ~60s, exit ~5min) plus owner override — exactly what consumers read from Redis. 0 Normal · 1 Elevated · 2 Critical.

panels.title: Level (preview, raw)
panels.description: Instantaneous threshold verdict (ae:pressure_level:preview recording rule) with NO hysteresis. When this flaps while the governor holds steady, the hysteresis is doing its job.

panels.title: Level over time (when)
panels.description: Published (governor) degradation level across the selected range. Orange annotation markers = transitions from the ClickHouse history, labeled with from→to and the reasons.

options.content:
**What / when / why** for graceful degradation (spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-10-graceful-degradation-design.md`).

- **When** — governor level timeline (left); orange annotations = transitions with reasons (ClickHouse `degradation_transitions`, 365d).
- **Why** — *Active reasons* below names what justifies the CURRENT level; *Breached signals* shows the raw threshold verdicts; host + bandwidth rows show the underlying signals.
- **What** — *Heavy actors* row = shed candidates (Camoufox, library torrent/encode, upscaler). Phase 3 pauses their admission at Elevated+.
- **Hysteresis**: enter after ~60s sustained breach, exit after ~5min clean — the preview stat flapping while the governor holds = working as intended.
- **Override**: `bin/degradation-override.sh set 0|1|2` pins the level (reason `manual_override`); `clear` releases. Consumers read Redis `ae:degradation:level` (TTL'd — dead governor fails open to Normal).
- **Thresholds** (Elevated → Critical): PSI cpu `some` 25→45% · PSI io `full` 15→30% · PSI mem `full` 5→15% · MemAvailable 10→5%. Swap is context-only (chronically ~90% on this box by design).

panels.title: Active reasons (governor)
panels.description: Which reasons currently justify the published level (ae_degradation_reason_active). Yellow = elevated-tier signal, red = critical-tier, blue = info (manual_override / prometheus_unreachable).

panels.title: Breached signals (preview thresholds, raw)
panels.description: Instantaneous threshold verdicts per signal, before hysteresis. Yellow rows = elevated-tier breaches, red rows = critical-tier. Compare with 'Active reasons' above to see what the governor accepted as sustained.

panels.title: Shed state (what is actually paused)
panels.description: ae_degradation_shed as reported by the consumers themselves: library download/encode claim gates + storyboard backfill, and the Camoufox sidecar (1 = new-work admission paused at Elevated, 2 = refusing at Critical). Scheduler skips are momentary — see scheduler_job_skipped_total{reason="degraded"} instead.

panels.title: Pressure score (governor vs preview)
panels.description: The governor's EWMA-smoothed pressure score (ae_degradation_score{job="governor"}, authoritative — drives the graduated caps) vs the instantaneous, unsmoothed preview (ae:pressure_score:preview). Threshold lines at 0.40 (Elevated-tier caps start biting) and 0.80 (Critical-tier caps).

panels.title: Per-signal score
panels.description: Per-signal contribution to the pressure score (ae:pressure_score:signal), one series per signal label. Shows which host/bandwidth signal is driving the overall score up.

panels.title: PSI — pressure stall ratios
panels.description: Kernel pressure-stall information: share of wall-clock time tasks were stalled waiting for the resource. 'some' = at least one task stalled; 'full' = all non-idle tasks stalled. These are the primary degradation triggers.

panels.title: CPU utilization / load per core
panels.description: Context signals (not triggers): overall CPU busy ratio and 5-minute load average divided by core count (8 cores). Load/core > 1 means runnable tasks exceed capacity.

panels.title: Memory available / swap used
panels.description: MemAvailable/MemTotal is a trigger (Elevated <10%, Critical <5%). Swap-used is context only — this box intentionally runs ~90% swap; memory PSI is the honest stall signal.

panels.title: Disk IO utilization (busiest device)
panels.description: Share of time the busiest block device spent doing IO. Sustained ~100% alongside io PSI = torrent/ffmpeg signature.

panels.title: Egress breakdown (bits/s)
panels.description: Pairs the vnstat uplink truth with per-consumer counters: playback (streaming HLS proxy — CRITICAL tier, must survive) vs torrent upload (HEAVY tier, shed first) vs everything else. 10m windows smooth vnstat's 5-minute buckets.

panels.title: Ingress (bits/s)
panels.description: Host download side: vnstat RX total vs the torrent download share (HEAVY tier).

panels.title: Camoufox RAM
panels.description: Combined RSS of the Camoufox/Firefox process tree (stealth_ram_bytes). Admission soft budget 2 GiB, hard 3 GiB; Phase 3 clamps the soft budget at Elevated.

panels.title: Camoufox pool / sessions
panels.description: Live browser profiles, free slots, and retained stream sessions. Phase 3 stops warming at Elevated and refuses new solves (kind=degraded) at Critical.

panels.title: Library activity
panels.description: Active torrents plus encodes completed per 10m. Phase 3 stops the workers claiming NEW download/encode jobs at Elevated (running ffmpeg always finishes).

panels.title: Upscaler queue depth
panels.description: Jobs by status in the upscaler lease queue. Finalize/mux runs local CPU; a later actuation wave can pause new segment leases.

panels.title: HLS proxy active connections (CRITICAL-tier demand)
panels.description: Live playback connections through the streaming proxy — the demand the degradation system protects. Rising connections + rising pressure = exactly when shedding should kick in.

panels.title: Library disk free
panels.description: Free bytes on the library volume. The existing disk guard (LIBRARY_DISK_FREE_MIN_PCT=20) already refuses new downloads below its floor — an actuation primitive this design builds on.

panels.title: content-verify worker cap
panels.description: Graduated probe-loop cap (content_verify_worker_cap, kind=pressure/demand/effective — effective = min(pressure, demand)) vs actual in-flight probe leases (content_verify_inflight_leases). Phase-derived: CV_CURVE maps pressure score to a cap; CV_DEMAND_PER_WORKER derives a demand-side cap from pending backlog.

panels.title: Camoufox pool
panels.description: Graduated warm-browser target (stealth_pool_target, from STEALTH_POOL_CURVE) vs retained sessions (stealth_active_sessions), overshoot (stealth_pool_over_target), and kill rate (increase(stealth_pool_kills_total[10m])) as the pool is shrunk to match target.
